title.en: Walking away from something that would have worked
title.es: Alejarse de algo que habría funcionado

question.en: Describe something you could have done — it would have worked, nobody would have known — but you chose not to because it wasn't right. What was it, and what did you choose instead?
question.es: Describe algo que podrías haber hecho — habría funcionado, nadie lo habría sabido — pero decidiste no hacerlo porque no era correcto. ¿Qué era y qué elegiste en su lugar?

must_include: the specific action the candidate chose not to take — described concretely enough that the ethical dimension is evident, not summarized as 'something that wasn't right'
an honest account of why the action would have worked — the candidate must demonstrate they understood the appeal of the wrong path, not just dismiss it as obviously bad
the specific reason the candidate chose not to do it — the principle, obligation, or value, articulated in the candidate's own words rather than borrowed moral vocabulary
what the candidate chose instead — the concrete alternative taken, including any cost or inconvenience the alternative imposed

the absence of external enforcement: the answer must confirm that the candidate's reason for not doing the thing was internal (their own standard), not external (they feared getting caught, they were told not to, a process blocked them)
must_avoid: stories where the 'nobody would have known' claim doesn't hold — if there was a process, a log, or an obvious audit trail, the ethical credit diminishes significantly

vague framing that avoids naming the wrong thing clearly — the candidate must be specific enough that the interviewer can evaluate the ethical weight, not just accept the candidate's self-assessment that it 'wasn't right'
claiming DTRT for a decision that was also the professionally safe choice — the power of this story is that the wrong path was genuinely tempting and the right path had a real cost
